title: sporobolus virginicus subsp. littoralis
intro: Sporobolus virginicus subsp. littoralis, also known as coastal dropseed, is a perennial grass native to the coastal regions of the southeastern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sporobolus virginicus subsp. littoralis include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe wounds and reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
